architecture_fit.note: Single hand-set attention head (x4), no MLP, minimal delta from base_model.py; matching emerges from a genuine softmax(Q·Kᵀ), not written in.

baseline_comparison.note: Both a recency-only strawman (depth ablated, A=0) and uniform chance plotted on the same axes; strawman collapses with depth while full circuit holds.

faithfulness.note: Hand-built, so faithfulness is a self-ablation: zeroing the depth term breaks matching — and the README honestly proposes the activation-patching check a trained model would need.

operating_range.note: Depth-invariant (~0.999) across the full goal axis d=1..5, but does not probe beyond the demo regime or locate a break point.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire circuit is hand-set QK/softmax weights reproducing the target behaviour — exactly the hand-built mechanism this bonus rewards.

visual_judgement.note: Heatmap with cyan true-match boxes for per-token verification plus a 3-line depth-sweep money plot make the claim and its strawman legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties heatmap to per-token check and the line plot's y-axis to the exact headline metric, x-axis to the goal's sweep axis, with the right baseline.

notes: A strong hand-built attempt: a minimal single-head, no-MLP delta from base_model.py where stack-matching emerges from a real softmax(Q·Kᵀ) over depth-match/is-open/recency features, hitting ~0.99 attention-to-match (chance ~0.07) and depth-invariant robustness ~0.999. The depth-ablation strawman and uniform baseline give clean causal/comparison evidence on shared axes, and the visualisation is well-justified. Only modest weakness is operating range — it covers the full goal axis (d=1..5) but never pushes past the demo regime to find where the circuit breaks.
